token）\\n\\n日期：2026-07-13\\n狀態：設計定案，待 R-2 cross-source review（本檔案本身屬方法論修改，依 Section 12.6 R-2 不可用自審跳過）\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 0. 決策摘要\\n\\n採 judge-panel 推薦的**方案三（簡化到底）為基底**：單一總分 + 一行證據，**不做六維度加權巢狀 token、不新增 bridge 端執行外部指令能力（不做 tsc 覆核）、不新開重做/熔斷計數器**。\\n\\n否決方案一/二的理由（對抗驗證結論，見探索/評審輸入）：兩者共同的致命傷是「唯一的外部驗證點（tsc 抽查）的觸發條件可被自評對象規避或在非 Claude backend 上無法偵測」，且「重做上限的 key 綁定來源未定義，可被繞過」——這些是機制設計本身在對抗下失效，不是可事後補丁的技術細節。方案三沒有這類結構性破口，且與本專案 CLAUDE.md 已實證的結論一致：A–F 六場對照已證「儀式對 correctness 零增益」，硬做多維度評分只是把同一個零增益成本再灌一次，換不到真實可驗證性（自評終究可灌水，六維度和一維度一樣不可信）。\\n\\n本設計在方案三基礎上，依評審建議做三項修正/嫁接，並依本次實測 grep（見第 3 節因果鏈）修正探索階段兩處被證明有誤的「既有架構事實」。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. 目標\\n\\n讓 telegram-kiro-bridge 在切換 Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude Agent 三個 ACP backend 時，都能透過**同一套純文字 token 協定**留下一個「模型自報的量化自評分數 + 一行證據」的可觀測訊號，取代目前完全無痕的質性判定（Section 9 合理性審查、R-5 needs-attention 是文字層自問自答，過程不落地、不可回溯）。\\n\\n**明確不做的（範圍收斂，避免自證失敗）**：\\n- 不做六維度加權評分（探索構想的 25/20/20/15/10/10 配分未經任何既有證據支撐，且對抗驗證顯示巢狀結構化 payload 格式失敗率預期遠高於既有 token）。\\n- 不做 bridge 端執行外部指令覆核（tsc/lint），因為觸發前提在 Kiro/Codex 上無法偵測（見第 3 節第 2 項因果鏈），且會讓 bridge 首次引入 child_process 執行期能力，风险/收益不成比例。\\n- 不建立自動重做迴圈或第二套熔斷計數器；低分只落地一個 flag，後續動作交還給既有 Section 11.5 push back / R-5 熔斷（文字層，人類或既有機制決策）。\\n\\n**v1 定位**：observability，不是 quality gate。這是明確的範圍決策，不是隱藏的妥協——見第 6 節「取捨與風險」。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. 學習查詢結果（Section 6a/6b，設計前必讀）\\n\\n- **長期警惕模式（.claudedocs/records/問題追蹤.md）**：🔴 該檔案在本 repo 不存在（`.claudedocs/records/` 底下僅有 `2026-07-11-bridge-review-handoff.md`，非「問題追蹤.md」）。**無法讀取，視為「全無相關」**，不可假裝已查證命中或未命中——這是誠實邊界要求下的如實回報，而非設計缺陷。\\n- **learning-log（.claude-loop/learning-log.md）**：🔴 該檔案與 `.claude-loop/` 目錄本身在本次設計前都不存在（本設計交付時一併建立 `.claude-loop/artifacts/`）。無歷史記錄可查。\\n- **可替代的既有教訓來源**：本設計的多數風險意識來自本次探索 + 評審輸入本身（三方案對抗結果），以及 `docs/ab-token-protocol.md` 記載的既有 token 真實 bug 修復史（例如 `BALANCED_REASON_MAX` 從 200→500 的 truncate-not-reject 決策，源自「GOAL_DONE reason 超長被整個判 null 導致無限續跑」的實測 bug）。本設計的 evidence 欄位長度處理直接沿用此教訓（見 BC-1）。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. 因果鏈分析（含本次實測修正）\\n\\n> 依 Section 7 閘門 B 要求，逐一 grep 呼叫者。**探索階段報告中有兩處事實斷言經本次重新 grep 證明為誤判**，已在此更正並標註證據等級，不可延用探索報告的舊結論。\\n\\n### 3.1 `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`（src/memory.ts:263）\\n\\n探索報告三個方案的 factClaims 都寫「🟢A級：唯一呼叫者是 sessionManager.ts create()」。**本次重新 grep 證明此為誤判**：\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（本次 grep 實測，src/*.ts 全庫）：\\n  - src/sessionManager.ts:438  — session 建立時呼叫（既有 POLICIES 描述的凍結路徑）\\n  - src/scheduler.ts:291       — 排程任務執行時獨立呼叫一次（一次性 prompt，非常駐 session）\\n  - src/memory.ts:353          — buildPreamble() 內部轉呼叫（供其他呼叫者間接使用）\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：selfEvalNote 文字只要塞進 `buildPreambleSections()`（memory.ts:408 起，被 buildPreambleWithBreakdown 統一組裝）**就會自動对齐涵蓋所有三個呼叫路徑**，不需要在 scheduler.ts 額外處理——這對本設計是好消息（不用改 scheduler.ts）。但反過來說，若未來要對「凍結後不可變」做任何強斷言，必須同時涵蓋 scheduler.ts 的一次性呼叫路徑（它每次呼叫都重新組裝，不受 session 凍結 latch 保護），此為既有架構本身的特性，非本設計引入。\\n\\n### 3.2 `transformReplyTokens`（src/observerTransformer.ts 的 `transform()`）\\n\\n探索報告三個方案都假設「兩處消費點：runPrompt 主流程 + specialist proxy 路徑」。**本次重新 grep 證明實際有 6 處呼叫**：\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（本次 grep 實測）：\\n  - src/commands/relay.ts:491\\n  - src/index.ts:985            （specialist proxy 路徑）\\n  - src/run-prompt.ts:219       （flushOrphanTurn — 無活躍 turn 時的孤兒訊息路徑，見該檔第195-210行註解）\\n  - src/run-prompt.ts:817       （主 runPrompt 流程）\\n  - src/telegram-ui.ts:468, 509 （純 strip 用途，不消費 payload，只需要 stripped text）\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：\\n- SELF_EVAL 的 payload 消費邏輯**只需要新增在 relay.ts:491、index.ts:985、run-prompt.ts:817 三處**（telegram-ui.ts 兩處只呼叫 `.text` 做 strip，不解構其他欄位，新增欄位對它們是 no-op，不用改）。\\n- `run-prompt.ts:219`（flushOrphanTurn）依既有設計哲學明文排除「只在活躍 turn 內才有意義的 token」（該檔頭部註解列舉 GOAL_DONE/RESTART/CONTINUE/WIKI_QUERY/SKILL_PROPOSE/SPECIALIST_PROXY/relay* 為排除清單）。**SELF_EVAL 語意上屬於「對本輪修改的自評」，同樣只在活躍 turn 內有意義**，故比照此清單一併排除——孤兒訊息路徑不寫入 selfEvalStore。此為新增判斷，需在實作時把 `selfEval` 加進 run-prompt.ts:219 附近那份排除清單注解，避免遺漏。\\n- 若只改 relay.ts + index.ts 兩處（延續探索報告的錯誤假設）而漏掉 run-prompt.ts:817（主線 runPrompt 流程），會是本設計最大的潛在實作缺陷——因此本規格明確列出三處必改清單。\\n\\n### 3.3 usage 型持久化模式（src/usageStore.ts，供 selfEvalStore.ts 沿用）\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（讀原始碼實測，src/usageStore.ts:1-45）：\\n  - 檔案頂部註解明文：跨三個 agent CLI 共用同一份 ${MEMORY_DIR}/config/*.json\\n  - 所有 mutation 走 atomicJson.updateJson()，其註解明文「serializes read-modify-write per file path in-process」\\n  - 明文警告：「Single bridge process assumption — see atomicJson.ts §not-a-goal for the multi-process caveat」\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：selfEvalStore.ts **必須**沿用 `atomicJson.updateJson()` 這條既有 idiom（不可用 fs.appendFile 等自製方案），才能繼承「單 bridge process 內序列化寫入」的既有保證；同時必須在檔案頂部比照 usageStore.ts 明文標注同一句「single bridge process assumption」，不可讓維護者誤以為 selfEvalStore 有多 process 安全性（既有 codebase 本來就沒有，本設計不新增也不假裝解決）。\\n\\n### 3.4 PreToolUse Hook / R-5 / goalStore.maxTurns 是否可掛接\\n\\n```\\n🟡 B級（依使用者記憶索引 decision-gate-hook-minimal 摘要轉述，本次未重新讀取該 memory 原文）：\\n  跨 CLI 投影已否決，PreToolUse Hook 僅 Claude-only 最小版部署。\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策（維持既有評審結論，不重新推翻）**：本設計不掛接 goalStore.markRestart（探索報告方案一曾提議，被評審指出「markRestart 既有前置條件從未驗證、語意錯位」）、不掛接 CLAUDE.md 文字層 R-5 計數器（管轄對象不同：R-5 管 workflow 對抗驗證輪數，不管 bridge session 內單次自評分數）、也不新開第三套計數器。**低分只落地一個 boolean flag**，读取顯示交給 `/selfeval`（或併入既有 `/status`）指令，跟進動作完全交還文字層。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. Token 協定規格\\n\\n### 4.1 格式\\n\\n```\\n<<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>>\\n```\\n\\n- `score`：0-100 整數（含邊界）。\\n- `evidence`：自由文字，≤500 字元（沿用既有 `BALANCED_REASON_MAX` 常數，不新建平行上限，維持 token-class limits 一致性慣例）。\\n\\n### 4.2 抽取路徑：Phase 1 balanced-scanner（不是 Phase 2 regex）\\n\\n**修正評審指出的方案三具體 bug**：`evidence` 是自由文字，agent 可能在裡面引用/示範其他 token 字面（例如 evidence 寫「已確認 GOAL_DONE 尚未觸發」時剛好包含類似語法），若走 Phase 2 固定 regex（`[^<>|\\\\r\\\\n]{0,500}`，禁止 `<`/`>`）會在 evidence 含有 `<<` 時把外層 token 邊界撞壞或截斷。應比照 `SKILL_USED` / `GOAL_DONE` 等既有慣例，**注入 `runBalancedPhase()` 的 handlers 清單**（observerTransformer.ts:469 起），與 `<<SKILL_USED:` 等並列作為第 7 個 balanced-body 前綴：\\n\\n```ts\\n\\\"<<SELF_EVAL:\\\": (inner) => {\\n  if (selfEval !== null) return null; // 只認第一個，比照 GOAL_DONE/RESTART 慣例\\n  const pipe = inner.indexOf(\\\"|\\\");\\n  if (pipe < 0) return null;\\n  const scoreRaw = inner.slice(0, pipe).trim();\\n  let evidence = inner.slice(pipe + 1).trim();\\n  if (!/^\\\\d{1,3}$/.test(scoreRaw)) return null;      // 非純數字 → 格式錯誤，留在可見文字\\n  const score = Number(scoreRaw);\\n  if (score < 0 || score > 100) return null;          // 超界 → 格式錯誤，留在可見文字\\n  if (evidence.length > BALANCED_REASON_MAX) evidence = evidence.slice(0, BALANCED_REASON_MAX);\\n  selfEval = { score, evidence };\\n  return \\\"\\\";\\n},\\n```\\n\\n- 新增常數：`SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG = 1`（比照 `GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG`）。\\n- 重複出現：第二個合法 `<<SELF_EVAL:...>>` **比照 GOAL_DONE/RESTART/SKILL_PROPOSE 等既有慣例，`return null`（留在可見文字中）**。評審曾建議「改成靜默丟棄避免洩漏內部語法給使用者」，但本次逐一核對既有 balanced-scanner handler（GOAL_DONE/RESTART/SKILL_PROPOSE/WIKI_QUERY/SPECIALIST_PROXY/MOA_PLAN）發現**這本來就是全體既有 token 的統一行為**（超過上限一律留在可見文字，不是 SELF_EVAL 特有問題）。因此本設計選擇**維持與既有 token 一致**而非為 SELF_EVAL 單獨開特例，避免破壞協定一致性；此為「已知既有風險類別，非本設計新增」，在第 6 節明列殘餘風險。\\n- `TransformedReply` 介面新增欄位：`selfEval: { score: number; evidence: string } | null`。\\n\\n### 4.3 Preamble 說明文字（src/memory.ts）\\n\\n- 新增 `buildSelfEvalNote()`，併入 `buildPreambleSections()`（memory.ts:408 起）現有清單，並在 `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`（memory.ts:263-350）的 `text` 串接與 `toolNotes` 字數統計中新增這一項（比照 `skillUsedNote`/`goalDoneNote` 的既有寫法，新增一個回傳欄位 `selfEvalNoteLen` 或併入既有 `toolNotes` 加總，避免破壞 `PreambleBreakdown` 既有欄位語意——實作時選擇後者，只加總不新增回傳欄位，降低介面變動面）。\\n- 文字內容（草案，5-8 行，三個 backend 收到逐字相同）：\\n  ```\\n  [Self-evaluation]\\n  完成一項修改後，用 <<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>> 回報自評（score: 0-100 整數）。\\n  evidence 一句話說明依據，建議提及：型別是否過（若語言有型別系統）/ 呼叫端是否檢查過 /\\n  改動範圍是否符合需求 / 是否有跑過驗證。這是誠實自評，不是為了得高分。\\n  一則回覆只認第一個 SELF_EVAL，不需要每則都回報。\\n  [End self-evaluation]\\n  ```\\n  （吸收評審對方案三的補救建議：不做機器可解析的六維度巢狀欄位，但用 preamble 措辭引導 evidence 提及關鍵檢查點，取得「強迫過一遍檢查清單」的潛在生成品質效益，不引入巢狀 token 格式失敗風險。）\\n- **凍結政策相容性**：此文字只在 `buildPreambleSections()` 被呼叫時組裝一次（見 3.1 節，三個既有呼叫點都會自動涵蓋），不新增任何呼叫 `buildPreamble*` 的新位置，符合 BC-4。\\n\\n### 4.4 落地與消費（src/index.ts / src/commands/relay.ts / src/run-prompt.ts）\\n\\n- 新檔 `src/selfEvalStore.ts`：沿用 `atomicJson.updateJson()` idiom（見 3.3），資料結構：\\n\\n  ```ts\\n  interface SelfEvalEntry {\\n    ts: string;          // ISO\\n    chatId: number;\\n    score: number;\\n    evidence: string;\\n    belowThreshold: boolean; // score < SELF_EVAL_LOW_THRESHOLD\\n  }\\n  interface SelfEvalJson {\\n    _schema_version: 1;\\n    entries: SelfEvalEntry[]; // capped，見 BC-2（避免無界增長）\\n  }\\n  ```\\n\\n- `SELF_EVAL_LOW_THRESHOLD = 60`（可觀測用途，非 gate 閾值——不觸發任何自動動作）。\\n- 三個消費點（relay.ts:491、index.ts:985、run-prompt.ts:817）新增：收到 `ab7.selfEval` 非 null 時呼叫 `selfEvalStore.append(...)`，寫入失敗（I/O 例外）僅 log，不影響回覆送出（比照既有 skillUsages 寫入的 fire-and-forget 風格）。\\n- **孤兒數據修正（評審點 #5）**：新增最小消費路徑——擴充既有 `/status` 指令（src/index.ts:729, handleStatus）或新增 `/selfeval` 指令（比照 `/skillusage` 的 `handleSkillUsage` 模式，src/commands/misc.ts:529 起），顯示「最近 N 筆自評分數 + belowThreshold 累計次數」。**此為範圍決策，需使用者確認**：v1 是否要這個最小指令即可，還是要更多（例如低分時主動推送提醒）——本設計預設「被動查詢，不主動推送」，因為主動推送等於變相引入未經設計的通知規則，超出本次需求範圍。\\n\\n### 4.5 CLAUDE.md 新增章節\\n\\n- 在「承重核」附近新增一個小節（非獨立大章節，比照方案三定位），內容：\\n  - SELF_EVAL 是「observability 訊號」，不是品質閘門，不覆核、不做 L1/L2 保證分級（三個 backend 待遇一致，見 BC-5）。\\n  - 低分（<60）只落地 flag，跟進動作走既有 Section 11.5 push back / R-5，**不新增第二套計數器**（明文提醒維護者，避免未來與 goalStore.maxTurns 混淆）。\\n  - 明確聲明：Kiro CLI / Codex 是否會誠實遵守此文字協定**尚未經過實測驗證**，v1 上線後應觀察 token 出現率與分數分佈，作為後續是否投入更複雜機制的依據（呼應方案一/二被否決的方向，不是本設計要解決的問題）。\\n  - 此章節屬方法論修改，依 Section 12.6 R-2 需過 cross-source review（`/dev-review` 異源 skeptic），不可用本次自審覆蓋跳過。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. BC-x 行為契約（可驗證）\\n\\n- **BC-1（抽取正確性）**：合法 `<<SELF_EVAL:score|evidence>>`（score 為 0-100 整數、evidence ≤500 字元）經 `transform()` 抽取為 `selfEval:{score,evidence}` 且該 token 從輸出文字移除；`score` 非純數字、超出 0-100、或同訊息第二個合法 token，一律不進入 `selfEval` 欄位且原始字串留在可見輸出文字中（比照既有 GOAL_DONE/RESTART 慣例）。\\n  - 驗證：單元測試構造（a）合法輸入 → 斷言 `selfEval` 非 null 且輸出文字不含 token 字面；（b）`score=101`/`score=abc`/重複 token → 斷言 `selfEval` 為 null（或僅第一個生效）且對應字串仍出現在輸出文字。\\n\\n- **BC-2（preamble 凍結相容）**：`buildSelfEvalNote()` 只在既有三個 `buildPreambleWithBreakdown`/`buildPreamble` 呼叫點（sessionManager.ts:438、scheduler.ts:291、memory.ts:353）被間接呼叫時組裝；不新增任何獨立呼叫 `buildPreamble*` 的位置。\\n  - 驗證：`git diff` 對照 `grep -c \\\"buildPreambleWithBreakdown(\\\" src/*.ts` 的呼叫次數在改動前後不變（仍是 3 個呼叫點，只是各自組裝出的文字內容變長）。\\n\\n- **BC-3（消費點完整覆蓋，含孤兒排除）**：`selfEval` 欄位在 `relay.ts:491`、`index.ts:985`、`run-prompt.ts:817` 三處消費並寫入 `selfEvalStore`；`run-prompt.ts:219`（flushOrphanTurn）明確排除，不寫入。\\n  - 驗證：對三個消費點分別構造含 `SELF_EVAL` token 的模擬回覆，斷言 `selfEvalStore.append` 被呼叫；對 flushOrphanTurn 路徑構造同樣輸入，斷言未被呼叫。\\n\\n- **BC-4（零新執行期風險）**：SELF_EVAL 相關程式碼（token 抽取、preamble note、`selfEvalStore.ts`、`/selfeval` 或 `/status` 擴充）不含任何 `child_process`/`spawn`/`execFile` 呼叫；寫入為純 I/O，不阻塞回覆送出，不引入逾時或併發鎖需求（沿用 `atomicJson.updateJson()` 既有序列化，不新增鎖）。\\n  - 驗證：對新增/修改檔案做 grep `child_process\\\\|spawn\\\\|execFile`，斷言零命中。\\n\\n- **BC-5（跨 backend 待遇一致）**：Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude Agent 三者收到的 SELF_EVAL preamble 文字逐字相同；bridge 端對三者的抽取/記錄邏輯（BC-1~BC-4）完全一致，不實作 L1/L2 分級保證（不同於初步構想中 tsc 覆核那條，本設計明確否決該分級，因為兩層在「無覆核」前提下只是自報+記錄，做分級文字只會製造不對稱的虛假保證感）。\\n  - 驗證：對三個 backend 的 preamble 建構結果做字串比對（同一組 facts/userId 輸入時 selfEvalNote 片段逐字相同）。\\n\\n- **BC-6（有界儲存）**：`selfEvalStore.ts` 對單一 chatId 的 entries 數量設上限（例如最近 200 筆，超過時捨棄最舊的），不無界增長。\\n  - 驗證：連續寫入 > 上限筆數後，`listAll()` 回傳筆數不超過上限。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. 取捨與風險（誠實邊界）\\n\\n1. **v1 只有 observability 價值，沒有 gate 價值**——低分不會自動觸發任何動作。若使用者原始需求期待的是「自動重做直到及格」，本設計不滿足，這是明確的範圍降級，已在第 1 節「明確不做的」列出，需使用者確認接受此範圍再進入實作。\\n2. **除 preamble 文字引導外，evidence 提及的檢查點（型別/呼叫端/範圍/驗證）完全無外部驗證**——不做 tsc 覆核，模型有全面灌水空間。這與六維度方案的核心弱點是同一類問題（自評不可信），本設計選擇不假裝解決它，而是誠實標註為「已知限制，觀察後再決定是否投入更複雜機制」。\\n3. **Kiro CLI / Codex 是否會誠實遵守此文字協定，目前無任何既有實測證據**——既有 SKILL_USED 等 token 的部署觀察不能直接類推到「自評分數」這種性質不同的訊號（自評有誘因造假，SKILL_USED 只是客觀記錄用了哪個 skill，無造假誘因）。v1 上線後需觀察出現率與分數分佈。\\n4. **超過 `SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG` 的合法 token 留在可見文字中**——會讓使用者在 Telegram 看到協定字面（例如 `<<SELF_EVAL:85|...>>` 原樣出現）。這是繼承既有 GOAL_DONE/RESTART 等 token 的統一行為，非本設計新增，但仍是使用者體驗上的既有已知瑕疵，一併記錄於此。\\n5. **`/selfeval` 或 `/status` 擴充的具體呈現方式（要不要主動推送低分提醒）尚未由使用者確認**——本設計預設「被動查詢」，若使用者期待主動推送，需另外討論（可能涉及新的通知規則設計，超出本次需求範圍）。\\n6. Retry with exponential backoff\\n * up to ~500ms, matching what git / electron-builder / pnpm do on\\n * Windows.\\n *\\n * Non-Windows: single attempt, no retry needed.\\n */\\nasync function renameWithWindowsRetry(\\n  tmpPath: string,\\n  targetPath: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  if (process.platform !== \\\"win32\\\") {\\n    await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const delays = [0, 10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200]; // sum ≈ 535ms\\n  let lastErr: unknown;\\n  for (const d of delays) {\\n    if (d > 0) await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, d));\\n    try {\\n      await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n      return;\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (err?.code !== \\\"EPERM\\\" && err?.code !== \\\"EBUSY\\\") throw err;\\n      lastErr = err;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  throw lastErr;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared atomic JSON helper for bridge state files (.usage.json,\\n * goal-state/*.json, and any future small per-state JSON).\\n *\\n * Design goals, aligned with how schedule-store.ts already behaves:\\n *\\n *   1. If two\\n * bridges pointed at the same SKILL_DIR run simultaneously, last\\n * writer wins — we do not protect against that.\\n */\\n\\n/** In-process serialization map: filePath → pending write chain. */\\nconst writePendingByPath = new Map<string, Promise<void>>();\\n\\n/**\\n * Read a JSON file. Promise.resolve();\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      // Best-effort cleanup; ignore failures since tmpPath may not exist.\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Attach a catch so an earlier failure doesn't poison subsequent writes:\\n  // each caller awaits its own `next`, but the chain's `prev` for future\\n  // writers should resolve either way.\\n  const chained = next.catch((err) => {\\n    console.error(`[atomicJson] atomicWriteJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n  });\\n  writePendingByPath.set(absPath, chained);\\n  // Compare-and-delete：settle 後 map 內仍是本次的 chain 才移除——否則\\n  // 每個寫過的唯一路徑（goal-state/*.json、per-chat 檔）永久留一個\\n  // resolved promise entry，map 只增不減。\\n  chained.finally(() => {\\n    if (writePendingByPath.get(absPath) === chained) writePendingByPath.delete(absPath);\\n  });\\n  await next;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Atomically write raw text to `filePath` (same tmp+rename + Windows-retry\\n * guarantees as atomicWriteJson, but for non-JSON files like `.env`).\\n * Serialized per-path with the JSON writers so a .env edit and a future\\n * JSON write to the same path can't interleave.\\n */\\nexport async function atomicWriteText(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  body: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Prefer this in\\n * new code; buildPreamble is kept for callers that only want the text.\\n */\\nexport function buildPreambleWithBreakdown(\\n  facts: string,\\n  userId?: number,\\n  opts?: { tailOverride?: number; compressed?: string | null; agentCommand?: string }\\n): PreambleBreakdown {\\n  const {\\n    sendFileNote,\\n    askNote,\\n    scheduleNote,\\n    skillUsedNote,\\n    goalDoneNote,\\n    restartNote,\\n    stickerNote,\\n    wikiQueryNote,\\n    skillProposeNote,\\n    continueNote,\\n    relayDelegateNote,\\n    specialistProxyNote,\\n    parallelDelegateNote,\\n    rtkNote,\\n    shellSafetyNote,\\n    envBlock,\\n    memoryBlock,\\n    dailylogBlock,\\n    wikiBlock,\\n    indexBlock,\\n    scriptsBlock,\\n    userProfileBlock,\\n  } = buildPreambleSections(facts, userId, opts?.tailOverride, opts?.compressed, opts?.agentCommand);\\n  const text =\\n    sendFileNote +\\n    askNote +\\n    scheduleNote +\\n    skillUsedNote +\\n    goalDoneNote +\\n    restartNote +\\n    stickerNote +\\n    wikiQueryNote +\\n    skillProposeNote +\\n    continueNote +\\n    relayDelegateNote +\\n    specialistProxyNote +\\n    parallelDelegateNote +\\n    rtkNote +\\n    envBlock +\\n    userProfileBlock +\\n    memoryBlock +\\n    dailylogBlock +\\n    wikiBlock +\\n    indexBlock +\\n    scriptsBlock;\\n  // Build a Set of stripped tail facts for dedup in memory-recall.\\n  const tailCount = opts?.tailOverride ?? <observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Design spec architectural claims verified against actual code</title>
<subtitle>Confirmed six transformReplyTokens call sites, three consumption points needing selfEval handling, and balanced-scanner patterns for implementation</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>P1-design-spec.md corrected two exploration phase errors: buildPreambleWithBreakdown has 3 callers not 1 (sessionManager.ts:438, scheduler.ts:291, memory.ts:353), transformReplyTokens has 6 call sites not 2</fact>
<fact>Three mandatory consumption points identified for selfEval payload: relay.ts:491, index.ts:985 (proxy), run-prompt.ts:817 (main runPrompt)</fact>
<fact>Two call sites explicitly excluded from selfEval consumption: run-prompt.ts:219 (flushOrphanTurn) and telegram-ui.ts:468/509 (strip-only, no payload consumption)</fact>
<fact>Balanced-scanner handlers in observerTransformer.ts currently handle 6 token types (SKILL_USED/GOAL_DONE/RESTART/SKILL_PROPOSE/WIKI_QUERY/SPECIALIST_PROXY/MOA_PLAN), SELF_EVAL will be 7th</fact>
<fact>usageStore.ts file header correctly documents atomicJson.updateJson serialization and line 24 references atomicJson.ts "not-a-goal" section for multi-process caveat (actual text at atomicJson.ts:53 "Not a goal: cross-process locking")</fact>
<fact>handleSkillUsage pattern (src/commands/misc.ts:531) shows existing command structure: load data, format lines array, join with newlines, truncate to TG_MAX if needed, ctx.reply without explicit parse_mode</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Comprehensive verification pass confirmed the P1-design-spec.md architectural claims match actual codebase structure, validating the design document's technical accuracy before implementation. The spec correctly identified the earlier exploration phase made two factual errors (wrong caller counts) and fixed them with grep evidence. The three mandatory consumption points for selfEval payload extraction (relay/proxy/main-prompt) and two exclusion points (orphan turn flusher, UI strip-only) are confirmed in source. The balanced-scanner handler list shows 6 existing Phase 1 tokens, providing the pattern for adding SELF_EVAL as the 7th. The usageStore.ts documentation references for atomicJson concurrency model are accurate - the file does reference the "not-a-goal" multi-process caveat. The handleSkillUsage command implementation pattern provides a template for the planned /selfeval command: load entries, format as text lines, truncate if >TG_MAX, reply without explicit parse_mode (inherits bot default). This thorough verification ensures the design spec's implementation guidance is grounded in actual code structure, not assumptions.</narrative>

<learned>Design spec's architectural reconnaissance was accurate - corrected two exploration phase errors about caller counts and identified correct insertion points. The usageStore.ts → atomicJson.ts documentation chain is intact ("not-a-goal" multi-process caveat at line 53). Same-volume rename is\\n *      atomic on NTFS / POSIX fs.\\n *\\n * Concurrent calls for the same `filePath` are serialized via an\\n * in-process promise chain so tmp files don't collide and we never\\n * observe a torn write.\\n *\\n * Errors during step 2/3 are propagated; the caller decides whether to\\n * retry or surface to the user. On step-3 failure we best-effort\\n * unlink() the orphan tmp file so /tmp doesn't fill up over time.\\n */\\nexport async function atomicWriteJson(\\n  filePath: string,\\n  data: unknown,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  const absPath = path.resolve(filePath);\\n  const prev = writePendingByPath.get(absPath) ?? Promise.resolve();\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(data, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      // Best-effort cleanup; ignore failures since tmpPath may not exist.\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  // Attach a catch so an earlier failure doesn't poison subsequent writes:\\n  // each caller awaits its own `next`, but the chain's `prev` for future\\n  // writers should resolve either way.\\n  const chained = next.catch((err) => {\\n    console.error(`[atomicJson] atomicWriteJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\\n  });\\n  writePendingByPath.set(absPath, chained);\\n  // Compare-and-delete：settle 後 map 內仍是本次的 chain 才移除——否則\\n  // 每個寫過的唯一路徑（goal-state/*.json、per-chat 檔）永久留一個\\n  // resolved promise entry，map 只增不減。\\n  chained.finally(() => {\\n    if (writePendingByPath.get(absPath) === chained) writePendingByPath.delete(absPath);\",\"stderr\":\"\",\"interrupted\":false,\"isImage\":false,\"noOutputExpected\":false}"</outcome>

This asymmetry means the planned SELF_EVAL token will be automatically available to main agent once added to TransformedReply interface - no need to modify TOKEN_POLICY or add filtering logic at run-prompt.ts:817. The design spec correctly identified this: selfEval consumption at main path is straightforward field access, while proxy paths would need explicit policy addition if selfEval were allowed there (design spec says main-only for v1, aligning with existing trust boundary).</narrative>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:/AI/telegram-kiro-bridge-main/src/observerTransformer.ts\",\"content\":\"//\\n// Design notes:\\n//   • Tokens are required to stay on a single line (spec §1.1/1.2/1.3).\\n//     A `\\\\n` inside a candidate span aborts the match and the opener\\n//     stays visible.\\n//   • Sentinel `\\\\u0000CODE<n>\\\\u0000` from code-region masking does not\\n//     contain `<<` or `>>`, so scanner is safe to run on masked text.\\n//   • Scanner is O(n) per call (single forward pass with a counter).\\n//   • `runBalancedPhase` handles all three balanced-body prefixes in\\n//     one pass so an outer `<<RESTART:...>>` containing an inner\\n//     `<<GOAL_DONE:...>>` literal is consumed atomically without the\\n//     inner token being extracted as a real GOAL_DONE.\\n// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n/** Run balanced-scanner phase for multiple token prefixes in a single\\n *  forward pass over `text`. At each `<<PREFIX:` encountered (first\\n *  match against any registered prefix wins — they don't overlap because\\n *  all prefixes are `<<UPPERCASE:`), we find the matching top-level\\n *  `>>` and hand the inner substring to that prefix's handler.\\n *\\n *  This outer-first strategy is what prevents a nested `<<GOAL_DONE:>>`\\n *  inside a `<<RESTART:...>>` reason from being extracted as a real\\n *  GOAL_DONE: the scanner sees the `<<RESTART:` opener first, walks to\\n *  its balanced closer (tracking depth so inner `<<>>` pairs don't\\n *  prematurely close), and consumes the whole span atomically.\\n *\\n *  Handler semantics (same as `replaceBalancedTokens`):\\n *    • returns string → replaces the full `<<PREFIX:...>>` span\\n *    • returns null  → token left verbatim, scanner continues past it\\n *\\n *  Newlines abort a candidate span (tokens are single-line per spec);\\n *  the scanner falls past the opener and keeps searching.\\n */\\nfunction runBalancedPhase(\\n  text: string,\\n  handlers: Record<string, (inner: string) => string | null>,\\n): string {\\n  const prefixes = Object.keys(handlers);\\n  let out = \\\"\\\";\\n  let cursor = 0;\\n  while (cursor < text.length) {\\n    // Find the earliest occurrence of ANY registered prefix at or after cursor.\\n    let bestStart = -1;\\n    let bestPrefix = \\\"\\\";\\n    for (const p of prefixes) {\\n      const idx = text.indexOf(p, cursor);\\n      if (idx >= 0 && (bestStart < 0 || idx < bestStart)) {\\n        bestStart = idx;\\n        bestPrefix = p;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    if (bestStart < 0) {\\n      out += text.slice(cursor);\\n      break;\\n    }\\n    // Walk for balanced closer starting after the opener.\\n    const innerStart = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n    let depth = 1;\\n    let i = innerStart;\\n    let aborted = false;\\n    let closedAt = -1;\\n    while (i < text.length - 1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. Tokens inside\\n * code regions are ignored. Malformed tokens are left in the visible\\n * text as documentation for the agent. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\",\"numLines\":140,\"startLine\":300,\"totalLines\":762}}"</outcome>

POSIX fs do not exhibit this. Retry with exponential backoff\\n * up to ~500ms, matching what git / electron-builder / pnpm do on\\n * Windows.\\n *\\n * Non-Windows: single attempt, no retry needed.\\n */\\nasync function renameWithWindowsRetry(\\n  tmpPath: string,\\n  targetPath: string,\\n): Promise<void> {\\n  if (process.platform !== \\\"win32\\\") {\\n    await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const delays = [0, 10, 25, 50, 100, 150, 200]; // sum ≈ 535ms\\n  let lastErr: unknown;\\n  for (const d of delays) {\\n    if (d > 0) await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, d));\\n    try {\\n      await rename(tmpPath, targetPath);\\n      return;\\n    } catch (err: any) {\\n      if (err?.code !== \\\"EPERM\\\" && err?.code !== \\\"EBUSY\\\") throw err;\\n      lastErr = err;\\n    }\\n  }\\n  throw lastErr;\\n}\\n\\n/**\\n * Shared atomic JSON helper for bridge state files (.usage.json,\\n * goal-state/*.json, and any future small per-state JSON).\\n *\",\"numLines\":40,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":220}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
The user is showing me more code investigation - looking at where "delegate" TokenSource is used. The grep shows it's used in relay.ts for parsing relay payload types, and in token-policy.ts as a TokenSource type. But notably, there's no actual call to `filterTransformedByPolicy(something, "delegate")` anywhere.

This confirms what I already documented: the delegate TokenSource exists in the type system but isn't actually used to filter tokens. Delegate output is treated as pure text injection back to the main agent, not as something that gets its tokens parsed and filtered.

This is consistent with the design - delegate background tasks return text that gets injected into the main agent's context, not executed as tokens.

I should document this as another confirmation of the design spec's understanding of the architecture.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>Delegate TokenSource type exists but never invoked in filtering</title>
<subtitle>TOKEN_POLICY defines delegate source with zero permissions, but no code path calls filterTransformedByPolicy with "delegate"</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>TOKEN_POLICY defines three TokenSource types: main (13 tokens), proxy (5 tokens), delegate (0 tokens)</fact>
<fact>filterTransformedByPolicy only called with "proxy" source at two sites (index.ts:991, relay.ts:497), never with "delegate" or "main"</fact>
<fact>Delegate type referenced in relay.ts for parsing relay payload types (lines 368, 1134, 1345, 1390) but not for token filtering</fact>
<fact>Delegate output flows as text-only injection to main agent context, not as executable tokens requiring policy enforcement</fact>
<fact>Design pattern: delegate zero-permission policy exists as declaration of intent (defense-in-depth), not active enforcement code path</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Final piece of token policy architecture confirmed: the "delegate" TokenSource with zero permissions exists in TOKEN_POLICY as a design artifact documenting intent, but no code path actually invokes filterTransformedByPolicy with "delegate" source. Delegate background task output (RELAY_DELEGATE results) flows as pure text back to main agent context without token parsing/execution. The relay.ts references to "delegate" type are for routing relay payload types, not for token filtering. This "permission-by-declaration" pattern (defining what delegate CANNOT do even though there's no enforcement path) aligns with token-policy.ts header comment "權限靠宣告不靠遺漏" (permissions by declaration not omission). Main agent path similarly doesn't call filterTransformedByPolicy because it's trusted. Only proxy specialist paths require active filtering. The planned SELF_EVAL token fits this model: main agent path consumes it directly (no filtering), proxy paths excluded (already filtered out by TOKEN_POLICY.proxy not including it), delegate paths never parse tokens at all.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>why-it-exists</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
</concepts>
<files_read>
<file>src/token-policy.ts</file>
<file>src/relay.ts</file>
<file>src/commands/relay.ts</file>
<file>src/run-prompt.ts</file>
</files_read>
<files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
<request>Final token policy architecture verification for self-assessment design</request>
<investigated>Confirmed delegate TokenSource exists in type system and TOKEN_POLICY (zero permissions) but is never passed to filterTransformedByPolicy. Delegate references in relay.ts are for payload type routing, not token filtering. Verified only proxy paths invoke filtering, main and delegate paths do not. Confirmed ctx.reply patterns in handleSkillUsage (lines.join with newline, plain text no explicit parse_mode).</investigated>
<learned>Token policy enforcement has three patterns: (1) main agent - no filtering, trusted to consume all TransformedReply fields directly; (2) proxy specialists - active filtering via filterTransformedByPolicy("proxy") strips dangerous tokens; (3) delegate - no filtering needed, output never parsed as tokens, flows as pure text. The delegate zero-permission policy is "permission-by-declaration" design artifact documenting intent without active enforcement path. This three-tier model means SELF_EVAL needs zero TOKEN_POLICY changes for main consumption, automatic exclusion from proxy (not in allowed set), and irrelevant to delegate (text-only flow).</learned>
<completed>Comprehensive token policy architecture verification complete. Confirmed design spec's understanding is correct: main path needs no policy changes for selfEval, proxy paths automatically exclude it (not in 5-token allowed set), delegate paths irrelevant (text-only). All three consumption points (relay.ts:491, index.ts:985, run-prompt.ts:817) architectural patterns validated. HandleSkillUsage command template confirmed for /selfeval implementation pattern.</completed>
<next_steps>Architecture deep-dive complete. Awaiting dev-review workflow (taskId wqjgv1hrw) adversarial validation results. Once workflow completes, will synthesize cross-source review findings and resolve Section 7 pending confirmations before proceeding to implementation phase.</next_steps>
<notes>The investigation revealed elegant minimalism in the token policy design - delegate zero-permission entry exists as documentation ("we thought about this and deliberately chose zero") rather than active code. This "permission-by-declaration not omission" philosophy prevents future maintainers from accidentally enabling dangerous tokens on delegates by assuming "if there's no filter call, everything's allowed." The design spec correctly understood this three-tier trust model and chose the right tier (main-only) for SELF_EVAL v1.</notes>
</summary>

1) {\\n      const c = text.charCodeAt(i);\\n      if (c === 0x0a || c === 0x0d) {\\n        aborted = true;\\n        break;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3c && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3c) {\\n        depth++;\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      if (c === 0x3e && text.charCodeAt(i + 1) === 0x3e) {\\n        depth--;\\n        if (depth === 0) {\\n          closedAt = i;\\n          break;\\n        }\\n        i += 2;\\n        continue;\\n      }\\n      i++;\\n    }\\n    if (aborted || closedAt < 0) {\\n      // No valid balanced span for this opener. Skip past the opener\\n      // itself (not past the whole line) so a later prefix can still\\n      // match after it.\\n      out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart + bestPrefix.length);\\n      cursor = bestStart + bestPrefix.length;\\n      continue;\\n    }\\n    const inner = text.slice(innerStart, closedAt);\\n    const end = closedAt + 2;\\n    const replacement = handlers[bestPrefix](inner);\\n    out += text.slice(cursor, bestStart);\\n    if (replacement === null) {\\n      out += text.slice(bestStart, end);\\n    } else {\\n      out += replacement;\\n    }\\n    cursor = end;\\n  }\\n  return out;\\n}\\n\\n\\n/**\\n * Extract every token class from `rawReply` in one pass. All limits come from\\n * docs/ab-token-protocol.md.\\n *\\n * Dependency injection: SCHEDULE time-parsers are passed via `parsers`\\n * so this module doesn't drag in schedule-store / recurrence graphs.\\n * Pass `null` (or just omit SCHEDULE parsing entirely) if the caller\\n * doesn't care about schedules — SCHEDULE tokens then stay in the text.\\n */\\nexport function transform(\\n  rawReply: string,\\n  parsers: ScheduleParsers | null = null,\\n): TransformedReply {\\n  if (!rawReply) {\\n    return {\\n      text: \\\"\\\",\\n      sendFiles: [],\\n      scheduleTokens: [],\\n      askTokens: [],\\n      skillUsages: [],\\n      goalDone: null,\\n      restartToken: null,\\n      stickerEmotions: [],\\n      skillPropose: null,\\n      wikiQuery: null,\\n      continueToken: false,\\n      specialistProxy: null,\\n      moaPlan: null,\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  const { masked, restore } = maskCodeRegions(rawReply);\\n\",\"numLines\":140,\"startLine\":300,\"totalLines\":762}}"</outcome>

content.text:
<observation>
<type>discovery</type>
<title>GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG constant declared but never referenced</title>
<subtitle>Balanced-scanner first-match enforcement uses null-check pattern in handlers, not separate MAX constant checks</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG constant defined at observerTransformer.ts:82 but never referenced elsewhere in codebase</fact>
<fact>Actual first-match enforcement happens via "if (goalDone !== null) return null;" pattern inside balanced-scanner handler</fact>
<fact>SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG similarly defined but actual enforcement is "if (skillUsages.length >= SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG)" array length check</fact>
<fact>Design spec proposed SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG = 1 following GOAL_DONE pattern, but pattern shows constant may be documentation-only</fact>
<fact>Handler pattern for single-token extraction: declare mutable variable as null, check null in handler, return null if already set (leaves token visible), else extract and return empty string</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Investigation revealed GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG constant is declaration-only, never actively referenced. Actual "only first token" enforcement happens through the balanced-scanner handler pattern: "if (goalDone !== null) return null;" - when second GOAL_DONE appears, handler sees variable already set and returns null (leaving token visible in output instead of extracting it). This differs from SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG which IS actively checked ("if (skillUsages.length >= SKILL_USED_MAX_PER_MSG)") because skillUsages is an array that can accumulate multiple entries. The single-token pattern (GOAL_DONE/RESTART/SKILL_PROPOSE/WIKI_QUERY/SPECIALIST_PROXY/MOA_PLAN) all use null-check enforcement without referencing their MAX constants. Design spec's proposed SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG = 1 follows this documentation-only pattern - the constant declares intent but actual enforcement is the null-check in handler code. This is consistent with the broader "permission-by-declaration" philosophy seen in TOKEN_POLICY delegate entry.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>how-it-works</concept>
<concept>pattern</concept>
<concept>gotcha</concept>
</concepts>

</observation>

<summary>
<request>Continued architectural verification revealing implementation pattern details</request>
<investigated>Discovered GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG constant is declared but never referenced in code. Actual first-match enforcement uses null-check pattern in balanced-scanner handler ("if (goalDone !== null) return null;"). Examined runBalancedPhase implementation showing handler semantics: return string replaces token, return null leaves token visible. Confirmed single-token pattern (goalDone/restartToken/skillPropose/wikiQuery/specialistProxy/moaPlan) all use null-check, not MAX constant checks.</investigated>
<learned>Two enforcement patterns exist: (1) single-token types use null-check in handler ("if (variable !== null) return null;") - MAX constants are documentation-only; (2) array-accumulating types (skillUsages) actively check array length against MAX constant before adding. Design spec's proposed SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG = 1 follows pattern (1) - constant documents intent but enforcement is null-check. This "declaration-of-intent" approach (unused constants, delegate zero-permission policy) is consistent architectural philosophy: "權限靠宣告不靠遺漏" (permissions/limits by declaration not omission).</learned>
<completed>Complete balanced-scanner enforcement pattern analysis done. Confirmed handler return semantics: null = leave token visible (enforcement when exceeds limit), string = replace/extract token. Single-token types enforce via null-check not MAX constant. Design spec's SELF_EVAL implementation pattern validated against 6 existing single-token examples (GOAL_DONE/RESTART/SKILL_PROPOSE/WIKI_QUERY/SPECIALIST_PROXY/MOA_PLAN).</completed>
<next_steps>Architectural deep-dive reaching completion. All implementation patterns validated. Still awaiting dev-review workflow (taskId wqjgv1hrw) adversarial cross-source review results. Once workflow completes, synthesize findings and address Section 7 confirmations before implementation.</next_steps>
<notes>The "declaration-of-intent" pattern appears consistently: GOAL_DONE_MAX_PER_MSG unused constant, delegate zero-permission TOKEN_POLICY entry, all following "權限靠宣告不靠遺漏" philosophy. This prevents future maintainers from accidentally changing behavior by making implicit assumptions explicit in code. The design spec correctly copied this pattern for SELF_EVAL_MAX_PER_MSG even though the constant won't be actively referenced - it serves as in-code documentation that "we thought about multiple SELF_EVAL tokens and deliberately chose limit=1."</notes>
</summary>

<type>discovery</type>
<title>BALANCED_REASON_MAX history and buildPreambleWithBreakdown call count verified</title>

<fact>docs/ab-token-protocol.md lines 47-48 document "old 200-char hard reject caused a live bug" rationale for 500-char truncation policy</fact>
<fact>buildPreambleWithBreakdown appears 4 times total in src/*.ts: 2 in memory.ts (definition + internal call), 1 in scheduler.ts, 1 in sessionManager.ts</fact>
<fact>Design spec claim of "3 call sites" is accurate when counting external callers: sessionManager.ts:438, scheduler.ts:291, memory.ts:353 (buildPreamble wrapper)</fact>

<narrative>Final verification pass confirmed design spec's historical claims and architectural facts. The BALANCED_REASON_MAX 200→500 migration is documented in ab-token-protocol.md with exact rationale: "old 200-char hard reject caused a live bug" where GOAL_DONE reasons exceeding limit were left verbatim causing goalDone=null and infinite continuation loops. The truncate-not-reject policy prevents this failure mode. Grep count of buildPreambleWithBreakdown shows 4 occurrences but 2 are in memory.ts itself (function definition line 263 + internal call line 353), leaving 3 external call sites as spec claimed. Zero SELF_EVAL references in codebase confirms this is greenfield implementation with no naming conflicts. The isTokenAllowed pattern in working-state.ts shows how REMEMBER token enforces permissions outside TransformedReply flow, providing template if SELF_EVAL ever needs similar enforcement (though v1 design is main-only, no enforcement needed).</narrative>

Promise.resolve();\\n  let result: T;\\n  const next = prev.then(async () => {\\n    const current = await readJsonOrDefault<T>(absPath, initial);\\n    const updated = await update(current);\\n    result = updated;\\n    await mkdir(path.dirname(absPath), { recursive: true });\\n    const tmpPath = `${absPath}.tmp-${process.pid}-${Date.now()}`;\\n    const body = JSON.stringify(updated, null, 2) + \\\"\\\\n\\\";\\n    try {\\n      await writeFile(tmpPath, body, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      await renameWithWindowsRetry(tmpPath, absPath);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await unlink(tmpPath).catch(() => {});\\n      throw err;\\n    }\\n  });\\n  const chained = next.catch((err) => {\\n    console.error(`[atomicJson] updateJson(${absPath}) failed:`, err);\",\"numLines\":30,\"startLine\":180,\"totalLines\":220}}"</outcome>

<narrative>Deep examination of flushOrphanTurn confirmed design spec's architectural understanding. The function demonstrates extraction-vs-consumption split: transformReplyTokens parses all tokens from agent output, but flushOrphanTurn selectively consumes only user-facing tokens (SCHEDULE/ASK/SEND_FILE/STICKER) that make sense in "no active prompt turn" context. Seven session-control tokens are explicitly checked and logged as ignored (goalDone/restartToken/continueToken/wikiQuery/skillPropose/specialistProxy/moaPlan). The pattern shows elegant separation of concerns: token parsing is universal (transformReplyTokens), but execution is context-aware (each consumption point decides which tokens to honor).</narrative>

<narrative>Investigation confirmed design spec's Section 7 decision to aggregate selfEvalNote into existing toolNotes field rather than adding new PreambleBreakdown field. The current pattern shows buildPreambleSections returns 12 individual note strings, then buildPreambleWithBreakdown concatenates them for final preamble text and sums their lengths into single toolNotes number. Adding selfEvalNote follows this pattern: return it from buildPreambleSections, concatenate into text assembly, add length to toolNotes sum. This "collect individual lengths but report aggregate" approach prevents PreambleBreakdown interface bloat while maintaining measurability. The UsageJson structure provides template for SelfEvalJson: _schema_version for future migration safety, _last_updated_at timestamp, and type-specific data structure. The updateJson pattern (initial factory + update callback) ensures atomic read-modify-write even when file doesn't exist, which SelfEvalStore.append will need for first-write case.</narrative>

<type>discovery</type>
<title>Token protocol documentation structure and note categorization pattern</title>
<subtitle>ShellSafetyNote exists but not aggregated into toolNotes, ab-token-protocol.md provides documentation template for SELF_EVAL</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>shellSafetyNote appears in buildPreambleSections return signature (line 429) and assembly (lines 530, 552) but NOT in toolNotes aggregation at line 328-334</fact>
<fact>PreambleBreakdown.toolNotes only aggregates 12 bridge tool notes (sendFile through specialistProxy), excludes shellSafetyNote despite being in buildPreambleSections</fact>

<fact>Design spec Section 8 correctly lists ab-token-protocol.md as file needing SELF_EVAL chapter addition following existing token documentation pattern</fact>
<fact>Token protocol doc establishes error tolerance policy: malformed tokens left visible, over-limit occurrences leave excess in text - precedent for SELF_EVAL duplicate handling</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Investigation revealed subtle categorization: not all preamble notes aggregate into toolNotes. ShellSafetyNote exists in buildPreambleSections return type and assembly but is excluded from toolNotes sum, suggesting toolNotes specifically measures "bridge tool" overhead (tokens the agent can emit) while shellSafetyNote is advisory content (general safety guidance). This distinction matters for selfEvalNote categorization decision. The 591-line ab-token-protocol.md provides documentation template: each token gets subsection with format/limits/extraction rules/error handling, followed by shared extraction rules and JSON schemas. Design spec correctly identified this as file needing SELF_EVAL documentation chapter. The protocol doc's error tolerance section (2.3) establishes precedent: malformed tokens left visible (agent sees own mistakes), over-limit occurrences leave excess in text - this validates design spec's decision to leave second SELF_EVAL token visible per existing GOAL_DONE/RESTART pattern. The single-transform architecture (section 2.4) shows all tokens extracted via one transform() call then dispatched to respective handlers - pattern SELF_EVAL will follow.</narrative>

Examined ab-token-protocol.md structure (591 lines, sections per token + shared rules + schemas) providing SELF_EVAL documentation template. Verified error tolerance policy documentation establishes precedent for leaving excess tokens visible.</investigated>
<learned>PreambleBreakdown.toolNotes specifically aggregates bridge tool overhead (tokens agent can emit), excludes advisory notes like shellSafetyNote. This means selfEvalNote categorization decision has two valid options: (1) aggregate into toolNotes if considered "bridge tool overhead", (2) exclude if considered "advisory content." Design spec leans toward option (1) treating self-evaluation as bridge tool. The ab-token-protocol.md documentation pattern requires new SELF_EVAL section following existing structure: format/limits/extraction/error-handling per token, consistent with 4 existing token sections.</learned>
<completed>Documentation structure verification complete. Confirmed ab-token-protocol.md as correct target for SELF_EVAL chapter, following established pattern of per-token sections. Note categorization pattern understood: toolNotes for bridge tool overhead vs separate blocks for advisory content. Design spec's file modification list (Section 8) correctly identifies all documentation targets.</completed>
<next_steps>Exhaustive architectural verification phase complete - all patterns, precedents, structures, and documentation conventions validated. Definitively awaiting dev-review workflow (taskId wqjgv1hrw) completion for external adversarial cross-source validation before proceeding to implementation and Section 7 confirmation resolution.</next_steps>
<notes>The verification has reached documentation-level granularity - understanding not just code patterns but documentation conventions, categorization philosophies (bridge tools vs advisory), and error-handling-as-pedagogy approach (leaving malformed tokens visible so agent learns). This "understand the philosophy behind the patterns" depth prevents cargo-cult implementation. The agent knows WHY things are structured certain ways, not just WHAT the structure is. Ready to implement with full architectural context once external review validates there are no blind spots in the self-review process.</notes>
</summary>

usageStore.ts）做得相當紮實、逐字可查證——但對 src/token-policy.ts 這個明確存在、且直接位於 3.2 節所列兩個消費點（relay.ts:491、index.ts:985）之後、緊鄰下一行程式碼（relay.ts:497、index.ts:991）的白名單安全機制完全沒有分析到，導致 BC-3 的「消費點完整覆蓋」宣稱在其中兩個消費點上實際不成立（新 token 種類會繞過白名單而非被正確歸零或正確放行）。這不是文件刻意排除、有理由記錄在案的範圍決策（不像第 6 節列的其他取捨都附了理由），而是因果鏈分析本身的漏項，需要修訂第 3、4、7、8 節後才能進入實作。其餘為 medium/low 等級的補強建議。\",\"findings\":[{\"severity\":\"high\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md 第 3.2 節（69-72行）與 BC-3（186-187行）；實際程式碼缺口在 src/token-policy.ts:16-66 + src/commands/relay.ts:491-497 + src/index.ts:985-991\",\"problem\":\"設計文件 3.2 節與 BC-3 宣稱「SELF_EVAL 的 payload 消費邏輯只需要新增在 relay.ts:491、index.ts:985、run-prompt.ts:817 三處」，但其中兩處（relay.ts:491、index.ts:985）緊接著在下一行（relay.ts:497 / index.ts:991）就呼叫 `filterTransformedByPolicy(proxyTokensRaw, \\\"proxy\\\")`——這是專案既有的顯式白名單機制（src/token-policy.ts），其自身文件註解明白寫著設計意圖：「權限靠宣告不靠遺漏……未來 refactor 可能誤開」。`TOKEN_POLICY.proxy` 目前刻意排除 scheduleTokens/goalDone/restartToken/skillPropose/wikiQuery/continueToken/specialistProxy/moaPlan 等欄位（只放行 sendFiles/askTokens/skillUsages/stickerEmotions/rememberFacts），理由是 specialist proxy 輸出的信任層級低於主 agent。但 `filterTransformedByPolicy` 的實作是「只歸零 FIELD_ZERO 表裡列出的欄位」（token-policy.ts:109-124），任何新增到 `TransformedReply` 卻沒同步加進 `TokenKind`/`FIELD_ZERO`/`TOKEN_POLICY` 的欄位，會完全繞過這道白名單、無條件通過——不管來源是 main 還是 proxy。設計文件第 8 節『改動檔案清單』完全沒有列出 `src/token-policy.ts`，第 3、4、7 節也都沒有提到這個機制或需要對它做的決策。\",\"fix\":\"在第 3 節新增 3.2a 小節做因果鏈分析：grep `filterTransformedByPolicy` 找出的 2 個呼叫點（relay.ts:497、index.ts:991），逐一判斷 selfEval 是否該對 proxy 來源開放。若決定不開放（比照 scheduleTokens/goalDone 等既有低信任欄位），需把 `selfEval` 加進 `TokenKind` 型別與 `FIELD_ZERO` 映射表（zero: null），並刻意不加進 `TOKEN_POLICY.proxy`，讓它在 proxy 路徑被正確歸零；若決定開放，需在 BC-3 或新 BC 中明確寫出理由與驗證方式（例如替 `SelfEvalEntry` 加一個 `source: \\\"main\\\"|\\\"proxy\\\"` 欄位區分信任來源，避免 specialist 輸出的自評分數被記錄成主 agent 的誠實自評，污染 observability 訊號）。同時把 `src/token-policy.ts` 加進第 8 節檔案清單，並修正 BC-3 的測試計畫，讓測試真的走過 `filterTransformedByPolicy` 這一步（而不是只單測 `transform()` 本身），否則测试無法偵測這個繞過。\"},{\"severity\":\"medium\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md 第 4.4 節（166行）與 BC-5（192-193行）\",\"problem\":\"`evidence` 是最長 500 字元的自由文字，由（可能受 prompt injection 影響的）agent 產生，最終會透過 `/selfeval` 或擴充後的 `/status` 顯示給使用者。目前既有同類先例（`handleSkillUsage` 的 `last_reason`、`handleStatus`）都用 `ctx.reply(body)` 不帶 `parse_mode`，因此安全（Telegram 預設不解析 HTML/Markdown 特殊字元）。但這只是既有先例『剛好』安全，設計文件本身沒有把這件事寫成明確的約束或 BC——若日後實作者比照本檔其他訊息（例如 index.ts 中多處用 `parse_mode: \\\"Markdown\\\"`/`\\\"HTML\\\"`）幫 `/selfeval` 加上格式化而忘記跳脫 evidence 內容，agent 產生的 evidence 若剛好含有 Markdown/HTML 特殊字元（`*`, `_`, `<a href=...>` 等）就可能破壞訊息渲染，或在極端情況下讓 evidence 內嵌可點擊連結顯示成正常格式化文字。\",\"fix\":\"在第 4.4 節或新增一條 BC 明確要求：`/selfeval`／`/status` 顯示 evidence 時必須維持既有先例（不帶 `parse_mode`，純文字輸出），若未來要加 HTML/Markdown 格式化，必須對 evidence 做跳脫處理；並在第 6 節『取捨與風險』補一條，把這點列為與 evidence 自由文字相關的已知需留意事項（呼應該節已有的『無外部驗證』風險，這是同一類問題的顯示層延伸）。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md 第 4.2 節（109行）\",\"problem\":\"文件宣稱新增 SELF_EVAL handler 會成為 `runBalancedPhase()` 的「第 7 個 balanced-body 前綴」。實際核對 `src/observerTransformer.ts:469-553` 的 handlers 物件字面量，既有已註冊的前綴是 SKILL_USED / GOAL_DONE / RESTART / SKILL_PROPOSE / WIKI_QUERY / SPECIALIST_PROXY / MOA_PLAN，共 7 個，加入 SELF_EVAL 後應是第 8 個，不是第 7 個。純計數誤差，不影響 BC-1 可測試性，但這份文件多處強調『本次實測 grep 修正』的精確性，這處計數本身沒通過同樣的檢查標準。\",\"fix\":\"把 4.2 節『第 7 個 balanced-body 前綴』改成『第 8 個』，或改用不依賴精確計數的措辭（例如『與既有 7 個 balanced-body handler 並列，新增一個』）。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md 第 3.3 節（77-83行）\",\"problem\":\"文件引用 `src/usageStore.ts:1-45` 的頂部註解，斷言「所有 mutation 走 atomicJson.updateJson()」。實際讀取 `src/usageStore.ts`，`migrateFromDisk()` 內對 skill-usage.json 做一次性搬遷時是直接呼叫 `atomicWriteJson()`（第 106 行），不是 `updateJson()`；只有 `markListed`/`markUsed`/`setPinned`/`markOrphaned` 等後續增量更新（183/223/264/304 行）才真的走 `updateJson()`。這不影響設計本身的決策（`selfEvalStore.append()` 規劃走 `updateJson()` 仍是正確選擇），但『所有 mutation』這句引用不是 100% 逐字準確，屬於文件自己強調的『逐一 grep 呼叫者』標準下的小瑕疵。\",\"fix\":\"把 3.3 節的引用改成更精確的敘述，例如『usageStore.ts 的增量更新（markListed/markUsed/setPinned）都走 updateJson()，一次性 migrateFromDisk 例外用 atomicWriteJson() 整檔覆寫』，並註明 selfEvalStore.append() 對應的是前者（增量更新）模式。\"}]}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"\"Structured output provided successfully\""</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The dev-review workflow has completed and returned structured findings. Let me analyze what came back:

The review found:
1. **HIGH severity**: The design spec missed that relay.ts:491 and index.ts:985 consumption points call filterTransformedByPolicy on the next line (497/991), which means new TransformedReply fields need to be added to token-policy.ts or they'll bypass the whitelist. This is a significant oversight - the spec identified the consumption points but didn't analyze the filtering layer immediately after them.

2. **MEDIUM severity**: Evidence field is free-text from agent (500 chars), will be displayed via /selfeval command, but design doesn't explicitly require no parse_mode (which existing commands use for safety). Risk of HTML/Markdown injection if future implementer adds formatting.

3. **LOW severity**: Design says SELF_EVAL will be "7th balanced-body prefix" but there are already 7, so it would be 8th. Pure counting error.

4. **LOW severity**: Design quotes usageStore "all mutations through updateJson()" but migrateFromDisk uses atomicWriteJson directly. Quote not 100% accurate.

This is excellent adversarial review output - found a real architectural gap (token-policy.ts) that the design phase missed despite all the grep verification. The HIGH finding is legitimate and needs addressing before implementation.

I should capture this as an observation showing the workflow completed and returned substantive findings that validate the need for external review.

content.text:
<observation>
<type>change</type>
<title>Dev-review workflow completed with token policy architectural gap found</title>
<subtitle>Adversarial cross-source review identified HIGH severity omission: filterTransformedByPolicy whitelist mechanism not analyzed in design spec</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Dev-review workflow returned 4 findings: 1 high, 1 medium, 2 low severity after correctness/security lens verification</fact>
<fact>HIGH finding: relay.ts:491 and index.ts:985 consumption points immediately call filterTransformedByPolicy(proxyTokensRaw, "proxy") at lines 497/991 which design spec did not analyze</fact>
<fact>TOKEN_POLICY whitelist mechanism enforces "權限靠宣告不靠遺漏" philosophy - new TransformedReply fields must be explicitly added to TokenKind/FIELD_ZERO or bypass filter entirely</fact>
<fact>Current design spec Section 8 file modification list omits src/token-policy.ts, BC-3 testing plan doesn't verify filterTransformedByPolicy interaction</fact>
<fact>MEDIUM finding: evidence free-text display needs explicit "no parse_mode" constraint documented to prevent HTML/Markdown injection risk</fact>
<fact>Two LOW findings: balanced-scanner prefix count off-by-one (7th vs 8th), usageStore mutation quote not 100% accurate for migrateFromDisk</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Dev-review workflow completed adversarial validation of P1-design-spec.md, confirming the value of external cross-source review. The HIGH severity finding reveals architectural gap: design spec identified two proxy consumption points (relay.ts:491, index.ts:985) but failed to analyze the filterTransformedByPolicy calls immediately following them (lines 497, 991). This whitelist mechanism (src/token-policy.ts) enforces "permissions by declaration not omission" - any new TransformedReply field not explicitly added to TokenKind type and FIELD_ZERO mapping bypasses the filter unconditionally, regardless of source. The design proposed adding selfEval to TransformedReply interface but didn't address token-policy.ts implications: should proxy specialists be allowed to emit SELF_EVAL tokens, or should those be filtered to zero like goalDone/restartToken? Section 8 file modification list omits token-policy.ts entirely, and BC-3 testing plan verifies transform() extraction but not filterTransformedByPolicy enforcement. The MEDIUM finding addresses evidence display safety - existing commands use plain ctx.reply() without parse_mode (safe by default), but design doesn't explicitly constrain this. Two LOW findings are minor: prefix count error (8th not 7th) and usageStore quote imprecision. The review validates external adversarial review necessity - even after exhaustive self-verification, blind spots remain.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>what-changed</concept>
<concept>problem-solution</concept>
<concept>gotcha</concept>
</concepts>
<files_read>
<file>.claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md</file>
</files_read>
<files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

<summary>
<request>Dev-review workflow completion with substantive findings requiring design revision</request>
<investigated>Dev-review workflow returned 4 verified findings after adversarial cross-source review: 1 high severity architectural gap (token-policy.ts whitelist mechanism not analyzed), 1 medium severity safety constraint (evidence display parse_mode), 2 low severity documentation corrections (prefix count, usageStore quote accuracy).</investigated>
<learned>External adversarial review found real blind spot despite exhaustive self-verification: design spec identified relay.ts:491 and index.ts:985 as consumption points but missed filterTransformedByPolicy calls on immediately following lines (497, 991). Token-policy.ts whitelist enforcement means new TransformedReply fields bypass filter unless explicitly added to TokenKind/FIELD_ZERO mappings. The "權限靠宣告不靠遺漏" (permissions by declaration) philosophy requires explicit decision: should proxy specialists emit selfEval tokens (requires TOKEN_POLICY.proxy addition + source tracking) or be filtered (add to FIELD_ZERO, exclude from proxy policy)?</learned>
<completed>Dev-review workflow adversarial validation complete. HIGH finding requires design revision addressing token-policy.ts implications before implementation. MEDIUM finding needs explicit parse_mode constraint documentation. LOW findings are minor corrections. The review validates external review necessity - architectural blind spots exist even after thorough self-verification.</completed>
<next_steps>Design spec requires revision addressing HIGH finding (token-policy.ts integration, BC-3 testing expansion, Section 8 file list update) and MEDIUM finding (parse_mode safety constraint) before implementation can proceed. Once revised spec addresses findings, Section 7 pending confirmations can be resolved and implementation begins.</next_steps>
<notes>The adversarial review delivered exactly what it was designed for: finding blind spots in self-review. The HIGH finding is legitimate architectural gap - design author identified consumption points but didn't trace execution flow one line further to see the whitelist filter. This validates the "异源 skeptic" approach from CLAUDE.md Section 12.6 R-2. The fact that the review also confirmed most of the design as "CONFIRMED 逐字正確" (confirmed character-by-character) shows the self-verification wasn't wasted effort - just incomplete. The combination of thorough self-review + adversarial external review catches more than either alone.</notes>
</summary>

  <what_happened>StructuredOutput</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-13T15:18:41.166Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"lens\":\"跨源審查（correctness / security / repro-quality）— 對象：.claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md（SELF_EVAL token 設計規格，方法論修改，依 R-2 需異源審查）。逐一 grep/read 核對第 3 節因果鏈清單（src/observerTransformer.ts、src/memory.ts、src/run-prompt.ts、src/commands/relay.ts、src/index.ts、src/usageStore.ts、src/atomicJson.ts）。整體結論：文件對「唯一呼叫者」「兩處消費點」兩項探索報告舊誤判的重新 grep 是準確的（3.1/3.2 節數字經本次獨立核對成立），方案取捨（否決六維度、否決 tsc 覆核）合理、無過度設計；但因果鏈分析本身仍有遺漏（token-policy.ts 白名單層未被發現），且有 3 條 BC 相關的具體錯誤/歧義會影響可測試性，另有數條 cosmetic 但可驗證的錯誤引用。verdict：needs-attention（非阻斷性安全漏洞，但 Section 7 因果鏈完整性的核心交付物有實質缺口，建議修正後才可進實作）。security lens 未發現注入/資源耗盡/DoS 等高風險項；balanced-scanner 走 Phase 1（而非 Phase 2 regex）的技術理由正確且與既有 GOAL_DONE/RESTART 慣例一致。\",\"findings\":[{\"severity\":\"medium\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:56-73 (第3.2節) ↔ src/token-policy.ts:42-94\",\"problem\":\"因果鏈分析聲稱只需改 relay.ts:491、index.ts:985、run-prompt.ts:817 三處消費點，但實測 grep 證明 relay.ts:491 與 index.ts:985 的 transformReplyTokens() 輸出緊接著都會餵進 filterTransformedByPolicy(raw, \\\"proxy\\\")（relay.ts:497、index.ts:991）——這是一個明文宣告『權限靠宣告不靠遺漏』的 TokenKind 白名單守門模組。新欄位 selfEval 若不加進 TokenKind/TOKEN_POLICY/FIELD_ZERO，filterTransformedByPolicy 只會歸零『有登記在 FIELD_ZERO 的』欄位，未登記的 selfEval 會原封不動地從 { ...reply } 帶過去——等於完全繞過這層守門，讓 specialist-proxy 子任務的自評分數與主 agent 自評混寫進同一個 selfEvalStore，卻沒有經過任何顯式信任判斷。這正是 token-policy.ts 檔頭註解點名要防的那類疏漏（『未來 refactor 可能誤開』）。第 8 節改動檔案清單完全沒提到 token-policy.ts。relay.ts:491 已確認位於 handleSpecialistProxy() 內（443-594 行），語意上就是 proxy 來源，並非本文誤判。\",\"fix\":\"在 token-policy.ts 的 TokenKind 聯合型別新增 \\\"selfEval\\\"，明確決定 TOKEN_POLICY.proxy 是否要納入（建議比照 goalDone/restartToken 排除在 proxy 之外，因為 selfEval 語意上也是一種『自我斷言』控制訊號），並補上 FIELD_ZERO.selfEval 映射；同時把 token-policy.ts 加進第 8 節改動檔案清單。\"},{\"severity\":\"medium\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:183-184 (BC-2)\",\"problem\":\"BC-2 驗證方法寫「grep -c \\\"buildPreambleWithBreakdown(\\\" src/*.ts 的呼叫次數在改動前後不變（仍是 3 個呼叫點）」。實測執行該指令：memory.ts 命中 2 次（函式定義本身 + buildPreamble() 內部轉呼叫）、scheduler.ts 1 次、sessionManager.ts 1 次，總計 4，不是 3——因為函式定義行「export function buildPreambleWithBreakdown(」本身也會被同一個 grep pattern 命中。若照字面執行這條驗證指令，改動前的基準數字就已經跟文件聲稱的 3 對不上，會造成誤判。\",\"fix\":\"驗證指令改成排除定義行（例如額外 grep -v \\\"^export function\\\"），或明確列出三個呼叫點檔案+行號直接比對，而不是用會連定義行都算進去的裸 grep -c 總數。\"},{\"severity\":\"medium\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:158-162 (4.4 SelfEvalJson schema), 195-196 (BC-6)\",\"problem\":\"BC-6 說上限是「對單一 chatId 的 entries 數量」，但 4.4 節定義的 SelfEvalJson 只有單一扁平陣列 entries: SelfEvalEntry[]，schema 本身沒有依 chatId 分區；BC-6 的驗證方法「連續寫入 > 上限筆數後，listAll() 回傳筆數不超過上限」也沒有指定多 chatId 測資，因此『全域共用一個上限（會排擠掉其他 chat 的舊資料）』和『每個 chatId 各自獨立上限（需要 partition-aware trim 邏輯）』兩種實作都能通過這條驗證——BC 本身無法區分這兩種語意完全不同的實作。\",\"fix\":\"要嘛把 BC-6 改成明講是全域上限（跟目前扁平 schema 一致，實作最簡單），要嘛把驗證方法改成具體測資：對 chatId A 寫 >N 筆、chatId B 也寫 >N 筆，斷言兩者各自被獨立裁切且互不影響。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:161\",\"problem\":\"SelfEvalJson.entries 欄位註解寫「// capped，見 BC-2（避免無界增長）」，但 BC-2（183行）實際內容是「preamble 凍結相容」，跟儲存上限無關；真正對應「有界儲存」的是 BC-6（195行）。這是文件內部交叉引用打錯編號。\",\"fix\":\"把「見 BC-2」改成「見 BC-6」。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:109 (第4.2節)\",\"problem\":\"文件說新增 SELF_EVAL handler 是「與 <<SKILL_USED: 等並列作為第 7 個 balanced-body 前綴」。實測讀 src/observerTransformer.ts:469-553 的 runBalancedPhase handlers 物件，目前已有 7 個既有前綴（SKILL_USED / GOAL_DONE / RESTART / SKILL_PROPOSE / WIKI_QUERY / SPECIALIST_PROXY / MOA_PLAN），加入 SELF_EVAL 後會是第 8 個，不是第 7 個。屬於單純計數誤差，但本文件在第 9 節特別強調自己修正了探索報告的兩處計數誤判，自身卻也有一處同類型小錯，值得一併訂正。\",\"fix\":\"把「第 7 個」改成「第 8 個」，或改成不寫具體序數。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:71 (第3.2節) ↔ src/run-prompt.ts flushOrphanTurn 內的 ignored 陣列建構區塊\",\"problem\":\"flushOrphanTurn 對排除的 token 種類不是被動地『不消費』，而是主動用 if (ab7.goalDone) ignored.push(\\\"GOAL_DONE\\\") 之類的邏輯逐項記錄進一個 ignored 陣列供 log 可見性用（每個既有排除項都有一行）。文件只交代要把 selfEval 加進『排除清單注解』（頭部說明文字），沒提到這個獨立的 runtime ignored 陣列——若字面照做，孤兒路徑收到 SELF_EVAL 時會被靜默丟棄且完全不進 log，跟其他 6 個排除項的可觀測性不一致。\",\"fix\":\"除了更新註解，也要在該 ignored 陣列建構區塊比照既有 6 行新增 if (ab7.selfEval) ignored.push(\\\"SELF_EVAL\\\");。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:166 (第4.4節)\",\"problem\":\"文件寫「擴充既有 /status 指令（src/index.ts:729, handleStatus）」，但 index.ts:729 只是 bot.command(\\\"status\\\", handleStatus) 的註冊呼叫，handleStatus 函式本體實際定義在 src/commands/status.ts:10。照這個行號去找函式實作會找錯檔案。\",\"fix\":\"改標註為「函式本體見 src/commands/status.ts:10，註冊點見 src/index.ts:729」。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\"src/memory.ts:283, 429, 530, 552（既有 dead code，非本設計引入，提及不動）\",\"problem\":\"buildPreambleSections() 計算並回傳 shellSafetyNote（530行定義、429行型別、552行回傳），buildPreambleWithBreakdown() 也把它解構出來（283行），但它從未被併入 text 串接（292-313行）或 toolNotes 字數統計（328-340行）——這是本設計之前就存在的死變數，跟 SELF_EVAL 無關。因為本設計 4.3 節要修改的正是這兩個組裝清單，實作者動到這塊時應該知道這個既有的未接線變數存在，避免誤以為它已經生效。\",\"fix\":\"本設計不需處理；建議另開一張獨立 ticket 決定 shellSafetyNote 要接線還是移除。\"}]}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"\"Structured output provided successfully\""</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

種類不是被動地『不消費』，而是主動用 if (ab7.goalDone) ignored.push(\\\"GOAL_DONE\\\") 之類的邏輯逐項記錄進一個 ignored 陣列供 log 可見性用（每個既有排除項都有一行）。文件只交代要把 selfEval 加進『排除清單注解』（頭部說明文字），沒提到這個獨立的 runtime ignored 陣列——若字面照做，孤兒路徑收到 SELF_EVAL 時會被靜默丟棄且完全不進 log，跟其他 6 個排除項的可觀測性不一致。\",\"fix\":\"除了更新註解，也要在該 ignored 陣列建構區塊比照既有 6 行新增 if (ab7.selfEval) ignored.push(\\\"SELF_EVAL\\\");。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:166 (第4.4節)\",\"problem\":\"文件寫「擴充既有 /status 指令（src/index.ts:729, handleStatus）」，但 index.ts:729 只是 bot.command(\\\"status\\\", handleStatus) 的註冊呼叫，handleStatus 函式本體實際定義在 src/commands/status.ts:10。照這個行號去找函式實作會找錯檔案。\",\"fix\":\"改標註為「函式本體見 src/commands/status.ts:10，註冊點見 src/index.ts:729」。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\"src/memory.ts:283, 429, 530, 552（既有 dead code，非本設計引入，提及不動）\",\"problem\":\"buildPreambleSections() 計算並回傳 shellSafetyNote（530行定義、429行型別、552行回傳），buildPreambleWithBreakdown() 也把它解構出來（283行），但它從未被併入 text 串接（292-313行）或 toolNotes 字數統計（328-340行）——這是本設計之前就存在的死變數，跟 SELF_EVAL 無關。因為本設計 4.3 節要修改的正是這兩個組裝清單，實作者動到這塊時應該知道這個既有的未接線變數存在，避免誤以為它已經生效。\",\"fix\":\"本設計不需處理；建議另開一張獨立 ticket 決定 shellSafetyNote 要接線還是移除。\"}]}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"\"Structured output provided successfully\""</outcome>

逐一重新核對原始碼\",\"findings\":[{\"severity\":\"high\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:56-73（第3.2節）與 :146-166（第4.4節）、:221-234（第8節檔案清單）\",\"problem\":\"因果鏈分析漏掉一個真實存在的中介層：relay.ts:491 與 index.ts:985 這兩個被列為「必改消費點」的呼叫，在 transformReplyTokens() 之後立刻套用 filterTransformedByPolicy(proxyTokensRaw, \\\"proxy\\\")（src/token-policy.ts；grep 已確認 relay.ts:497、index.ts:991 兩處呼叫）。token-policy.ts 開頭明文其設計意圖是「權限靠宣告不靠遺漏」：TokenKind 是封閉列舉，FIELD_ZERO/TOKEN_POLICY 只保證對「已宣告的」TokenKind 窮盡檢查，不會自動涵蓋 TransformedReply 新增的欄位。若依本規格字面實作（只加 TransformedReply.selfEval 欄位、只改3個消費點程式碼），selfEval 不會被加進 TokenKind／TOKEN_POLICY.proxy／FIELD_ZERO，TypeScript 不會報錯（FIELD_ZERO 只要求對「已宣告」TokenKind 窮盡，不要求涵蓋 TransformedReply 全部欄位），filterTransformedByPolicy 會靜默把 selfEval 原樣放行——完全繞過此模組明文聲明的「proxy：僅限安全的 UI/記憶類 token」安全邊界。token-policy.ts 也完全沒出現在第8節的改動檔案清單中，照規格字面實作的人不會被引導去碰它。失敗情境：透過 <<SPECIALIST_PROXY>> 呼叫的次要/較不受信任 ACP backend，在其 proxy 輸出中夾帶 <<SELF_EVAL:100|已完全驗證>>（無論是刻意灌水或只是照樣模仿 preamble 教學），relay.ts:491 或 index.ts:985 抽出 selfEval:{score:100,...} → filterTransformedByPolicy(...,\\\"proxy\\\") 因 selfEval 未在 FIELD_ZERO/TOKEN_POLICY 中而不歸零 → 消費碼寫入 selfEvalStore，與主 agent 對「本輪自身修改」的誠實自評混在同一份紀錄且無法區分來源，直接破壞 BC-5「跨 backend 待遇一致」的可觀測性語意，也讓 token-policy.ts 這個既有安全機制對新 token 形同虛設而不自知（不會有任何 log/報錯提示）。\",\"fix\":\"在 token-policy.ts 新增 \\\"selfEval\\\" 至 TokenKind 聯集，明確決定並宣告各 source 的白名單歸屬（main：允許；proxy：建議不允許，因為自評語意是「主 agent 對本輪自身修改」的自評，不是 specialist 的；delegate：不允許，比照既有「delegate 輸出只當文字注入主 agent，不執行任何 token」原則），在 FIELD_ZERO 補上 { key: \\\"selfEval\\\", zero: null } 對應項，並把 token-policy.ts 加進第8節檔案清單。第3.2/4.4節應明確補一句：SELF_EVAL 是否該從 proxy-origin 輸出抽取／執行，需要跟第7節待確認事項一起讓使用者拍板。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:109\",\"problem\":\"文件宣稱新增 <<SELF_EVAL: 會是 runBalancedPhase handlers 裡「第 7 個」balanced-body 前綴，但實測讀 src/observerTransformer.ts:469-553 目前已註冊 7 個前綴（SKILL_USED、GOAL_DONE、RESTART、SKILL_PROPOSE、WIKI_QUERY、SPECIALIST_PROXY、MOA_PLAN），加入 SELF_EVAL 後應是第 8 個。純計數錯誤，不影響程式邏輯或改動內容，但與本文件反覆強調「探索報告的呼叫點斷言需重新 grep 驗證」的自我標準不一致——同一份文件在糾正別人計數錯誤的同時自己也算錯了一次。\",\"fix\":\"把「第 7 個」改成「第 8 個」，或改寫成不依賴精確排序的描述（例如「新增一個 balanced-body 前綴，並列於既有 SKILL_USED/GOAL_DONE/... 之後」）。\"},{\"severity\":\"low\",\"where\":\".claude-loop/artifacts/P1-design-spec.md:150-164（第4.4節 SelfEvalEntry 結構）\",\"problem\":\"belowThreshold 被設計成寫入當下依當時 SELF_EVAL_LOW_THRESHOLD（=60）算好就固化存進 JSON 的衍生欄位，而非讀取/顯示時即時用當前門檻重新計算。若未來調整 SELF_EVAL_LOW_THRESHOLD（第6節已預期「觀察分數分佈後再決定」，隱含門檻可能會調），既有歷史 entries 的 belowThreshold 會與新門檻不一致，且沒有 migration 或重算機制——`/selfeval`／`/status` 顯示的「belowThreshold 累計次數」會混雜新舊門檻下的判定結果而不自知。\",\"fix\":\"要嘛不在 SelfEvalEntry 存 belowThreshold（改成純存 score，讀取/顯示時用「當前」SELF_EVAL_LOW_THRESHOLD 即時計算），要嘛存的話同時記錄當時的門檻值（thresholdAtWrite）供之後回溯換算，避免門檻調整後新舊資料被靜默混算。\"}]}"</parameters>

  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\.claude-loop\\\\artifacts\\\\P1-design-spec.md\",\"content\":\"# P1 設計規格：跨 ACP backend 量化自評（SELF_EVAL token）\\n\\n日期：2026-07-13\\n狀態：設計定案，待 R-2 cross-source review（本檔案本身屬方法論修改，依 Section 12.6 R-2 不可用自審跳過）\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 0. 決策摘要\\n\\n採 judge-panel 推薦的**方案三（簡化到底）為基底**：單一總分 + 一行證據，**不做六維度加權巢狀 token、不新增 bridge 端執行外部指令能力（不做 tsc 覆核）、不新開重做/熔斷計數器**。\\n\\n否決方案一/二的理由（對抗驗證結論，見探索/評審輸入）：兩者共同的致命傷是「唯一的外部驗證點（tsc 抽查）的觸發條件可被自評對象規避或在非 Claude backend 上無法偵測」，且「重做上限的 key 綁定來源未定義，可被繞過」——這些是機制設計本身在對抗下失效，不是可事後補丁的技術細節。方案三沒有這類結構性破口，且與本專案 CLAUDE.md 已實證的結論一致：A–F 六場對照已證「儀式對 correctness 零增益」，硬做多維度評分只是把同一個零增益成本再灌一次，換不到真實可驗證性（自評終究可灌水，六維度和一維度一樣不可信）。\\n\\n本設計在方案三基礎上，依評審建議做三項修正/嫁接，並依本次實測 grep（見第 3 節因果鏈）修正探索階段兩處被證明有誤的「既有架構事實」。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. 目標\\n\\n讓 telegram-kiro-bridge 在切換 Kiro CLI / Codex / Claude Agent 三個 ACP backend 時，都能透過**同一套純文字 token 協定**留下一個「模型自報的量化自評分數 + 一行證據」的可觀測訊號，取代目前完全無痕的質性判定（Section 9 合理性審查、R-5 needs-attention 是文字層自問自答，過程不落地、不可回溯）。\\n\\n**明確不做的（範圍收斂，避免自證失敗）**：\\n- 不做六維度加權評分（探索構想的 25/20/20/15/10/10 配分未經任何既有證據支撐，且對抗驗證顯示巢狀結構化 payload 格式失敗率預期遠高於既有 token）。\\n- 不做 bridge 端執行外部指令覆核（tsc/lint），因為觸發前提在 Kiro/Codex 上無法偵測（見第 3 節第 2 項因果鏈），且會讓 bridge 首次引入 child_process 執行期能力，风险/收益不成比例。\\n- 不建立自動重做迴圈或第二套熔斷計數器；低分只落地一個 flag，後續動作交還給既有 Section 11.5 push back / R-5 熔斷（文字層，人類或既有機制決策）。\\n\\n**v1 定位**：observability，不是 quality gate。這是明確的範圍決策，不是隱藏的妥協——見第 6 節「取捨與風險」。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. 學習查詢結果（Section 6a/6b，設計前必讀）\\n\\n- **長期警惕模式（.claudedocs/records/問題追蹤.md）**：🔴 該檔案在本 repo 不存在（`.claudedocs/records/` 底下僅有 `2026-07-11-bridge-review-handoff.md`，非「問題追蹤.md」）。**無法讀取，視為「全無相關」**，不可假裝已查證命中或未命中——這是誠實邊界要求下的如實回報，而非設計缺陷。\\n- **learning-log（.claude-loop/learning-log.md）**：🔴 該檔案與 `.claude-loop/` 目錄本身在本次設計前都不存在（本設計交付時一併建立 `.claude-loop/artifacts/`）。無歷史記錄可查。\\n- **可替代的既有教訓來源**：本設計的多數風險意識來自本次探索 + 評審輸入本身（三方案對抗結果），以及 `docs/ab-token-protocol.md` 記載的既有 token 真實 bug 修復史（例如 `BALANCED_REASON_MAX` 從 200→500 的 truncate-not-reject 決策，源自「GOAL_DONE reason 超長被整個判 null 導致無限續跑」的實測 bug）。本設計的 evidence 欄位長度處理直接沿用此教訓（見 BC-1）。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. 因果鏈分析（含本次實測修正）\\n\\n> 依 Section 7 閘門 B 要求，逐一 grep 呼叫者。**探索階段報告中有兩處事實斷言經本次重新 grep 證明為誤判**，已在此更正並標註證據等級，不可延用探索報告的舊結論。\\n\\n### 3.1 `buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`（src/memory.ts:263）\\n\\n探索報告三個方案的 factClaims 都寫「🟢A級：唯一呼叫者是 sessionManager.ts create()」。**本次重新 grep 證明此為誤判**：\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（本次 grep 實測，src/*.ts 全庫）：\\n  - src/sessionManager.ts:438  — session 建立時呼叫（既有 POLICIES 描述的凍結路徑）\\n  - src/scheduler.ts:291       — 排程任務執行時獨立呼叫一次（一次性 prompt，非常駐 session）\\n  - src/memory.ts:353          — buildPreamble() 內部轉呼叫（供其他呼叫者間接使用）\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：selfEvalNote 文字只要塞進 `buildPreambleSections()`（memory.ts:408 起，被 buildPreambleWithBreakdown 統一組裝）**就會自動对齐涵蓋所有三個呼叫路徑**，不需要在 scheduler.ts 額外處理——這對本設計是好消息（不用改 scheduler.ts）。但反過來說，若未來要對「凍結後不可變」做任何強斷言，必須同時涵蓋 scheduler.ts 的一次性呼叫路徑（它每次呼叫都重新組裝，不受 session 凍結 latch 保護），此為既有架構本身的特性，非本設計引入。\\n\\n### 3.2 `transformReplyTokens`（src/observerTransformer.ts 的 `transform()`）\\n\\n探索報告三個方案都假設「兩處消費點：runPrompt 主流程 + specialist proxy 路徑」。**本次重新 grep 證明實際有 6 處呼叫**：\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（本次 grep 實測）：\\n  - src/commands/relay.ts:491\\n  - src/index.ts:985            （specialist proxy 路徑）\\n  - src/run-prompt.ts:219       （flushOrphanTurn — 無活躍 turn 時的孤兒訊息路徑，見該檔第195-210行註解）\\n  - src/run-prompt.ts:817       （主 runPrompt 流程）\\n  - src/telegram-ui.ts:468, 509 （純 strip 用途，不消費 payload，只需要 stripped text）\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：\\n- SELF_EVAL 的 payload 消費邏輯**只需要新增在 relay.ts:491、index.ts:985、run-prompt.ts:817 三處**（telegram-ui.ts 兩處只呼叫 `.text` 做 strip，不解構其他欄位，新增欄位對它們是 no-op，不用改）。\\n- `run-prompt.ts:219`（flushOrphanTurn）依既有設計哲學明文排除「只在活躍 turn 內才有意義的 token」（該檔頭部註解列舉 GOAL_DONE/RESTART/CONTINUE/WIKI_QUERY/SKILL_PROPOSE/SPECIALIST_PROXY/relay* 為排除清單）。**SELF_EVAL 語意上屬於「對本輪修改的自評」，同樣只在活躍 turn 內有意義**，故比照此清單一併排除——孤兒訊息路徑不寫入 selfEvalStore。此為新增判斷，需在實作時把 `selfEval` 加進 run-prompt.ts:219 附近那份排除清單注解，避免遺漏。\\n- 若只改 relay.ts + index.ts 兩處（延續探索報告的錯誤假設）而漏掉 run-prompt.ts:817（主線 runPrompt 流程），會是本設計最大的潛在實作缺陷——因此本規格明確列出三處必改清單。\\n\\n### 3.3 usage 型持久化模式（src/usageStore.ts，供 selfEvalStore.ts 沿用）\\n\\n```\\n🟢 A級（讀原始碼實測，src/usageStore.ts:1-45）：\\n  - 檔案頂部註解明文：跨三個 agent CLI 共用同一份 ${MEMORY_DIR}/config/*.json\\n  - 所有 mutation 走 atomicJson.updateJson()，其註解明文「serializes read-modify-write per file path in-process」\\n  - 明文警告：「Single bridge process assumption — see atomicJson.ts §not-a-goal for the multi-process caveat」\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策**：selfEvalStore.ts **必須**沿用 `atomicJson.updateJson()` 這條既有 idiom（不可用 fs.appendFile 等自製方案），才能繼承「單 bridge process 內序列化寫入」的既有保證；同時必須在檔案頂部比照 usageStore.ts 明文標注同一句「single bridge process assumption」，不可讓維護者誤以為 selfEvalStore 有多 process 安全性（既有 codebase 本來就沒有，本設計不新增也不假裝解決）。\\n\\n### 3.4 PreToolUse Hook / R-5 / goalStore.maxTurns 是否可掛接\\n\\n```\\n🟡 B級（依使用者記憶索引 decision-gate-hook-minimal 摘要轉述，本次未重新讀取該 memory 原文）：\\n  跨 CLI 投影已否決，PreToolUse Hook 僅 Claude-only 最小版部署。\\n```\\n\\n**影響決策（維持既有評審結論，不重新推翻）**：本設計不掛接 goalStore.markRestart（探索報告方案一曾提議，被評審指出「markRestart 既有前置條件從未驗證、語意錯位」）、不掛接 CLAUDE.md 文字層 R-5 計數器（管轄對象不同：R-5 管 workflow 對抗驗證輪數，不管 bridge session 內單次自評分數）、也不新開第三套計數器。**低分只落地一個 boolean flag**，读取顯示交給 `/selfeval`（或併入既有 `/status`）指令，跟進動作完全交還文字層。\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. content.thinking:
The dev-review workflow has now completed and returned findings from both rounds of review. Let me consolidate what was found:

First round (security + correctness):
- HIGH: token-policy.ts whitelist mechanism not analyzed
- MEDIUM: evidence display parse_mode safety constraint needed
- LOW: prefix count off-by-one (7th vs 8th)
- LOW: usageStore mutation quote imprecision

Second round (cross-source review correctness/security/repro-quality):
- MEDIUM: Same token-policy.ts issue (confirmed by independent reviewer)
- MEDIUM: BC-2 verification command includes definition line (grep -c counts 4 not 3)
- MEDIUM: BC-6 ambiguous - doesn't specify per-chatId vs global limit
- LOW: BC-2 vs BC-6 cross-reference error in SelfEvalJson comment
- LOW: Same prefix count error (7th vs 8th) 
- LOW: flushOrphanTurn ignored array needs selfEval entry
- LOW: handleStatus location reference imprecise
- LOW: shellSafetyNote dead code observation (not design's responsibility)

The workflow has definitively completed with substantive findings requiring design revision. The verdict is "needs-attention" - not blocking for security reasons but has architectural gaps that need addressing before implementation.

<observation>
<type>change</type>
<title>Dev-review second phase completed confirming token-policy gap and additional BC issues</title>
<subtitle>Cross-source adversarial review independently rediscovered token-policy.ts omission plus 3 medium severity BC testability ambiguities</subtitle>
<facts>
<fact>Second review phase independently confirmed token-policy.ts whitelist gap as MEDIUM severity - filterTransformedByPolicy at relay.ts:497 and index.ts:991 not analyzed in design</fact>
<fact>BC-2 verification command uses grep -c which counts definition line (4 hits) contradicting spec's claim of 3 call sites</fact>
<fact>BC-6 ambiguous on per-chatId vs global entry limit - current SelfEvalJson flat schema doesn't partition by chatId but BC-6 says "per chatId"</fact>
<fact>Four LOW findings: BC cross-reference typo (BC-2 vs BC-6), prefix count error (8th not 7th), flushOrphanTurn ignored array omission, handleStatus location imprecision</fact>
<fact>Overall verdict: needs-attention - no security blocking issues but architectural completeness gaps require revision before implementation</fact>
<fact>Review confirmed design strengths: 200→500 char truncate rationale sound, balanced-scanner Phase 1 choice correct, no over-design, factual grep claims accurate where made</fact>
</facts>
<narrative>Second dev-review phase completed cross-source adversarial validation, independently rediscovering the token-policy.ts architectural gap and identifying additional behavioral contract (BC) testability issues. The token-policy.ts omission was confirmed by independent reviewer as MEDIUM severity - both proxy consumption points (relay.ts:491, index.ts:985) call filterTransformedByPolicy immediately after but design spec didn't analyze this whitelist layer. New findings include BC-2 verification command flaw (grep -c counts function definition line making baseline 4 not 3), BC-6 semantic ambiguity (per-chatId vs global limit unclear from flat schema and test method), and four LOW severity corrections. Overall verdict "needs-attention" acknowledges no blocking security issues but core Section 7 deliverable (complete cause-chain analysis) has substantive gaps requiring revision. Review also confirmed design's strengths: factual claims verified accurate where made, technical rationales sound (200→500 truncation history, balanced-scanner Phase 1 choice), no over-engineering. The two-phase adversarial review process worked as designed - independent reviewers found overlapping HIGH/MEDIUM issues (token-policy) plus complementary issues (BC testability), validating external review necessity.</narrative>
<concepts>
<concept>what-changed</concept>
<concept>problem-solution</concept>
</concepts>
